qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary code execution via JEXL expression evaluation with unrestricted permissions in nocode instrumentation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable component and root cause: this finding concerns nocode YAML-driven JEXL evaluation with unrestricted permissions, while the advisory concerns RMI instrumentation registering a network-reachable endpoint that deserializes incoming data without serialization filters.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described RMI instrumentation unsafe deserialization root cause. The only final qualifying finding targets nocode instrumentation JEXL expression evaluation from attacker-controlled YAML configuration, which is a separate source, sink, component, and control-flow condition from the RMI custom endpoint deserialization flaw described by GHSA-h8w2-rv57-vc6f.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-h8w2-rv57-vc6f describes Splunk OTel Java agent RMI instrumentation registering a custom endpoint that deserializes network input without serialization filters.
Session manifest lists one final finding: jexl_rce_via_unrestricted_nocode.
jexl_rce_via_unrestricted_nocode/vulnerability_report.json describes JexlEvaluator.java using JexlPermissions.UNRESTRICTED for nocode YAML expressions, not RMI instrumentation deserialization.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.
